home.heroSubtitle: I'm an engineering student in computer science at ENSEIRB-MATMECA, specializing in AI applied to computer vision. I explore and enhance systems to make them more interpretable and reliable—or design new ones altogether.

about.title: About Me
about.intro: I am currently a computer and artificial intelligence engineering student at ENSEIRB-MATMECA in Bordeaux, following the engineer-doctor pathway with a clear ambition to pursue AI research.
about.section1Title: An Unconventional Journey Driven by Passion
about.section1p1: My passion for computing began very early. At 12 years old, I discovered Scratch; at 13, I taught myself Python; and by 15, I enrolled in a technical high school. I quickly realized that what fascinated me most was algorithms, more than web development or game creation. I experimented for a while—robotics, development, Arduino projects—before finding a true calling in artificial intelligence.
about.section1p2: That spark came during an AI internship at Cali Intelligences, a startup specializing in computer vision and AI. It was a revelation. I then threw myself wholeheartedly into AI studies, determined to actively contribute to its advancement, especially through designing and refining deep learning model architectures.
about.section2Title: A Determination to Pivot and Excel
about.section2p1: Originally enrolled in a practice-oriented program, I made the bold decision to transition to academia to strengthen my theoretical foundation in mathematics and algorithms. I joined ENSEIRB-MATMECA, renowned for its academic rigor and its computer science track, despite numerous doubts surrounding my application. My determination paid off: I was admitted on merit, proving that motivation and self-learning can stand shoulder to shoulder with the most traditional paths.
